title: Daily English Practice
description: Short daily episodes to improve your English

episodes.title: Morning Routine Vocabulary
episodes.description: Words and phrases for talking about your morning

transcript.text: Good morning! Let's learn about morning routines.
I wake up at 7 AM. Then I brush my teeth.
After that, I take a shower and get dressed.
I have breakfast and then head to work.

episodes.title: Small Talk at Work
episodes.description: How to make conversation with colleagues

transcript.text: Today we'll practice small talk for the workplace.
Common starters: How was your weekend? How's your day going?
You can talk about weather, weekends, or current events.
Keep it light and friendly. Avoid controversial topics.

episodes.title: Giving and Receiving Compliments
episodes.description: How to give sincere compliments and respond naturally

transcript.text: Welcome to today's episode — compliments in English!
Giving a compliment: That looks great on you! You did an amazing job!
Responding to a compliment: Thank you! That's so kind. or Oh, it's nothing really.
In British English, people often downplay compliments: Oh, I just got lucky.
In American English, responses are often more direct: Thanks, I worked really hard on it!
Returning a compliment: Thank you! I love your outfit too!
Practise by giving one genuine compliment to someone today.

episodes.title: Expressing Emotions
episodes.description: Key vocabulary for describing how you feel

transcript.text: Hello! Today we're talking about emotions in English.
Basic emotions: happy, sad, angry, scared, surprised, disgusted.
More nuanced: anxious, overwhelmed, content, frustrated, excited, nervous.
Using them in sentences: I feel overwhelmed with work this week.
Asking about someone's feelings: Are you okay? You seem a bit down today.
Expressing strong feelings: I'm over the moon! or I'm absolutely furious!
Building emotional vocabulary is key to authentic English communication.

episodes.title: Agreeing and Disagreeing Politely
episodes.description: Phrases for expressing agreement and polite disagreement

transcript.text: Today's episode: how to agree and disagree politely in English.
Strong agreement: Absolutely! I couldn't agree more. Exactly right.
Partial agreement: That's a fair point, but... I see what you mean, however...
Polite disagreement: I'm afraid I don't quite agree. With respect, I think...
Avoid blunt disagreement: Not saying 'No, you're wrong' — soften it instead.
Asking for opinions: What do you think? Do you agree? How do you feel about that?
Master these phrases for confident, professional discussions in any setting.

episodes.title: Describing Your Job
episodes.description: How to talk about your work and career in English

transcript.text: Welcome! Today you'll learn how to describe your job in English.
Start with: I work as a... or My job title is... or I work in the...
Describe your responsibilities: I'm responsible for... My role involves...
Talk about your workplace: I work for a tech company. I'm based in the city centre.
Mention your team: I work in a team of ten. I report to the marketing director.
Discuss what you enjoy: The best part of my job is... I find it rewarding because...
Great! Now you can describe your career confidently to anyone.

episodes.title: Making Requests Politely
episodes.description: Learn polite ways to ask for things in English

transcript.text: Good morning! Today we focus on making polite requests.
Formal requests: Could you please...? Would you mind...? I would appreciate it if...
Informal requests: Can you...? Could you...? Would you...?
In shops: Could I have a coffee, please? I'd like two tickets to London.
At work: Could you send me the report by Friday? Would you mind reviewing this?
Adding please makes any request more polite in English.
Practice forming requests in your daily conversations today!

episodes.title: Talking About the Weekend
episodes.description: Common phrases and vocabulary for weekend conversations

transcript.text: Hello everyone! Today we practise talking about the weekend.
Ask: How was your weekend? Did you do anything fun?
Responses: I had a great time! I went to the cinema. I stayed at home and relaxed.
Describing activities: I went hiking. I caught up with old friends. I cooked a new recipe.
Expressing how you felt: It was exhausting but worth it. I really needed that break.
Plans for next weekend: I'm thinking of... I'm not sure yet. Probably just relax.
Talking about your weekend is perfect for practising past tense in English!
